messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BioC2026 Schedule Posted; Registration Open for Conference and Post-Conference Activities
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] BioC2026 Schedule Posted; Registration Open for Conference and Post-Conference Activities
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,
Please see the following updates for BioC2026:

  *   The schedule<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/schedule/> is now available on the BioC2026 website.
  *   Registration is open for both the conference and the post-conference events<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/post-conference/> (Carpentries Workshop and Hackathon).

Please take a look at the schedule and register if you?re planning to attend. Early bird registration pricing ends on June 15th.
More details are available on the BioC2026 website<https://bioc2026.bioconductor.org/>.
Best regards,
Laurah Ondari
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.body_plain:

nebbiolo1 specifically during checking? On r-universe [0], there 
are
BiocParallel issues on Linux.

I can't reproduce neither locally nor on Github [1].

Laurent

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] MSnbase timeout
messages.body_plain:
Hi Laurent,

I recently ran into the opposite problem of an inexplicable parallel test timeout with GitHub Actions that I was able to workaround by disabling testthat's parallel tests using the environmental variable TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=false (see https://github.com/ImmuSystems-Lab/toppgene/commit/b3ede460d6f106ddf32b131f2d194fc36bc15b1f); it may be worth trying out if one were to assume an underlying race condition or other stochasticity with testthat parallel tests. Not a great workaround, but, as you say, hard because to reproduce :/

Pariksheet

________________________________________

Sent: Monday, May 4, 2026 1:03 PM
To: Bioc-Devel List
Subject: [Bioc-devel] MSnbase timeout


Hello - any idea or input regarding the recent MSnbase timeouts on
nebbiolo1 specifically during checking? On r-universe [0], there
are
BiocParallel issues on Linux.

I can't reproduce neither locally nor on Github [1].

Laurent

[0]
https://github.com/r-universe/bioc/actions/runs/25096610349/job/74234361041
[1] https://github.com/lgatto/MSnbase/actions/runs/25329303978

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] miRBaseVersions.db and miRNAmeConverter package maintainer
messages.body_plain:
Hi Bioconductor Team,

I hope you are keeping well. I am the package maintainer of the two
packages miRBaseVersions.db (annotation package) and miRNAmeConverter.

Since the last update I have swapped laptops at least three times and have
no idea anymore how to update the two packages. I read through the git
guidelines but I'm not quite sure.

Could you please help me set up SSH again and let me know how to push my
latest changes from my git repo to your server?

Thanks and kind regards,
Stefan

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.body_plain:
I get a build error on devel [1] with R version 2026-04-17 r89917 [2] on netbiolo2 but I can't reproduce it locally with up-to-date packages and any of these development R versions:
2026-05-04 r89998
2026-04-20 r89917
2026-04-19 r89916
2026-04-19 r89915

Q1) netbioldo2 R r89917 is a bit old; how often is the devel branch R version updated?

Q2) Do failed package build automatically get re-run until they pass, in case their failures are dependency related?
Or is there a way to trigger a re-run without bumping the package version?
I ask because I wonder if it was a dependency version issue given the original error with several S4Vectors functions in the call trace:

* checking tests ...
  Running ?testthat.R?
 ERROR
Running the tests in ?tests/testthat.R? failed.
Last 13 lines of output:
    9.   ??methods::callNextMethod(y, ...)
   10.   ? ??base::eval(call, callEnv)
   11.   ?   ??base::eval(call, callEnv)
   12.   ??S4Vectors (local) .nextMethod(y, ...)
   13.     ??S4Vectors (local) .local(x, ...)
   14.       ??S4Vectors:::evalqForSubset(subset, x, ...)
   15.         ??S4Vectors:::evalArg(substitute(expr), envir, ..., where = parent.frame())
   16.           ??S4Vectors:::.find_arg_enclos(as.character(expr), where = where)
   17.             ??S4Vectors (local) matched_call(which)
   18.               ??base::match.call(...)
  
  [ FAIL 1 | WARN 0 | SKIP 0 | PASS 169 ]
  Error:
  ! Test failures.
  Execution halted

[1] https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.24/bioc-LATEST/toppgene/nebbiolo2-checksrc.html
[2] I'm not sure what's causing the 3 day mismatch in the date listed before the SVN revision.

Pariksheet

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] miRBaseVersions.db and miRNAmeConverter package maintainer
messages.body_plain:
You should log into your BiocCredentials account

https://git.bioconductor.org/BiocCredentials/login/?next=/BiocCredentials/

You should add a ssh key there fore access; it will be applied to your Bioconductor account.

You will then need to push your changes to the devel (and maybe RELEASE_3_23 if bug correct) branches of Bioconductor with a valid version bump for it to propagate.

Let us know if you have any further questions

Cheers


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

I hope you are keeping well. I am the package maintainer of the two
packages miRBaseVersions.db (annotation package) and miRNAmeConverter.

Since the last update I have swapped laptops at least three times and have
no idea anymore how to update the two packages. I read through the git
guidelines but I'm not quite sure.

Could you please help me set up SSH again and let me know how to push my
latest changes from my git repo to your server?

Thanks and kind regards,
Stefan

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Unreproducible build error with toppgene on devel branch on netbiolo2
messages.body_plain:
I'm not sure about the error but to answer your questions:

Q1)  The current cycle of release/devel, Bioc devel will also be on the release version of R (until the fall release when we will start using R-devel in prep for the Spring R release).  It should be the release candidate version of R 4.6 but we will likely update soon to the release version.

Q2) On our current system nebbiolo2, all packages are installed, built, and checked fresh daily.  So there will be a new run on today's report.


Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to de-deprecate ChIPQC
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor core team,

I'm writing to request de-deprecation of the ChIPQC package.

ChIPQC was deprecated because its Nozzle.R1 CRAN dependency was
archived and no longer installable, causing R CMD INSTALL to fail.


*I've committed a fix (version 1.49.1, on the devel branch,
commitc4b49cd) *that
removes advanced reporting but keeps the computation of all of the metrics.
This fix:

1. Formally deprecates the ChIPQCreport() function per Bioconductor's
standard 3-cycle deprecation guidelines. ChIPQCreport() now calls
.Deprecated() and returns invisibly; man/ChIPQC-deprecated.Rd points users
at the QC accessor methods (QCmetrics, etc.) and plotting of
individual metrics in the help files.

2. Removes Nozzle.R1 from Imports: and NAMESPACE.

3. Preserves the original ChIPQCreport.R implementation verbatim at
inst/legacy/report.r for possible future revival on a different
report-generation backend.

4. Fixes a latent Collate: field issue surfaced by R 4.6's strict
enforcement, plus several pre-existing R CMD check NOTEs.

R CMD check is clean: 0 ERRORs, 0 WARNINGs; 3 short NOTEs remain, all
pre-existing and unrelated to the deprecation work. These are tracked for
follow-up and don't affect package functionality. The core QC functionality
to compute metrics ? ChIPQC(), ChIPQCsample(), QCmetrics(),
coveragehistogram(), crosscoverage(), etc. ? is unaffected and fully
functional.

Note: because ChIPQC is currently marked as deprecated, it doesn't
appear in the public devel build report. The CHECK status reported above is
from macOS (aarch64-apple-darwin23, R 4.6 / Bioc 3.24), including building
the vignette. I also
verified Linux aarch64 cross-platform compatibility against
bioconductor/bioconductor_docker:devel: all 22 R/Bioc dependencies install
cleanly, the package loads (with the .onAttach deprecation banner firing as
expected), and ChIPQCsample() runs end-to-end producing valid QC output.

I have not removed PackageStatus: Deprecated or the .onAttach() deprecation
banner ? those changes await your approval.

Please let me know if you need any further information, or if I should push
these fixes to the release branch.

Thank you,
Rory Stark
bioconductor at starkhome.com

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:

I'm writing to request de-deprecation of the ChIPQC package.

ChIPQC was deprecated because its Nozzle.R1 CRAN dependency was
archived and no longer installable, causing

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Maintainer change for DenoIST
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Team,

I am the current maintainer of the Bioconductor package DenoIST. We would like to change the maintainer to be Amelia Dunstone. Her contact information is as follows:

Email: amelia.dunstone at svi.edu.au
ORCID: 0009-0009-6426-1529
GitHub ID: dunstone-a

Please let me know if there is anything else required from my end. Thank you!

Regards,
Aaron Kwok



Mr Aaron Kwok BBiomedSc - MPhil

Research Assistant


[https://www.svi.edu.au/images/svi_email_logo.png]<https://svi.edu.au/>
Mob             0452648921

Web             svi.edu.au<https://www.svi.edu.au/>

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to de-deprecate ChIPQC
messages.body_plain:
The package was deprecated in 3.23 and removed from 3.24 you are correct that they currently do not appear or are building on devel.  Please push your changes to RELEASE_3_23 branch.  Once it builds there cleanly without ERROR we will undeprecate the package.


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

ChIPQC was deprecated because its Nozzle.R1 CRAN dependency was
archived and no longer installable, causing R CMD INSTALL to fail.


*I've committed a fix (version 1.49.1, on the devel branch,
commitc4b49cd) *that
removes advanced reporting but keeps the computation of all of the metrics.
This fix:

1. Formally deprecates the ChIPQCreport() function per Bioconductor's
standard 3-cycle deprecation guidelines. ChIPQCreport() now calls
.Deprecated() and returns invisibly; man/ChIPQC-deprecated.Rd points users
at the QC accessor methods (QCmetrics, etc.) and plotting of
individual metrics in the help files.

2. Removes Nozzle.R1 from Imports: and NAMESPACE.

3. Preserves the original ChIPQCreport.R implementation verbatim at
inst/legacy/report.r for possible future revival on a different
report-generation backend.

4. Fixes a latent Collate: field issue surfaced by R 4.6's strict
enforcement, plus several pre-existing R CMD check NOTEs.

R CMD check is clean: 0 ERRORs, 0 WARNINGs; 3 short NOTEs remain, all
pre-existing and unrelated to the deprecation work. These are tracked for
follow-up and don't affect package functionality. The core QC functionality
to compute metrics ? ChIPQC(), ChIPQCsample(), QCmetrics(),
coveragehistogram(), crosscoverage(), etc. ? is unaffected and fully
functional.

Note: because ChIPQC is currently marked as deprecated, it doesn't
appear in the public devel build report. The CHECK status reported above is
from macOS (aarch64-apple-darwin23, R 4.6 / Bioc 3.24), including building
the vignette. I also
verified Linux aarch64 cross-platform compatibility against
bioconductor/bioconductor_docker:devel: all 22 R/Bioc dependencies install

expected), and ChIPQCsample() runs end-to-end producing valid QC output.

I have not removed PackageStatus: Deprecated or the .onAttach() deprecation

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Deadline Extended: EuroBioC2026 Call for Late-Breaking Abstracts and registration
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Deadline Extended: EuroBioC2026 Call for Late-Breaking Abstracts and registration
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor community,

There?s still time to join EuroBioC2026 in Turku this June! The deadline for late-breaking abstract submissions and conference registration has been extended to 18 May 2026.

Alongside the scientific programme, attendees will also have the chance to experience a Finnish summer evening at the conference dinner on the island of Ruissalo. Inspired by Juhannus, the evening will include Finnish cuisine, an optional sauna, informal networking, and outdoor activities at the historic Villa Marjaniemi.

? Submit your abstract here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdBkwTH84uIzaMk5Zy5kxdQnSkoDbaUGdmcbFD007hpL8i-cg/viewform
?? Register for the conference here, if you haven't already: https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/pages/registration.html

Best regards,
Laurah Ondari,
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BiocAsia Seminar Series
messages.body_plain:
We're pleased to announce the first in the planned BiocAsia Seminar Series
beginning on the 12th May, as a way to help those in the Asia Pacific
region more easily connect with the Bioconductor community. The seminar
will be held at UTC+600, which corresponds to these key time zones in the
Asia-Pacific: 1600 (AEST), 1530 (ACST) 1500 (JST) 1400 (CST) 1130 (IST). We
have two brilliant presenters lined up to help us launch as well.

Prof Matt Ritchie (WEHI, Melbourne, Australia) "Developing Bioconductor
tools for long-read sequencing analysis"

Dr Helena Crowell (CNAG, Barcelona, Spain) ?Building community and
infrastructure for spatial omics in R/Bioconductor?

Please register at
https://adelaide.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_vF6K27DgRRGVhG_HYRaSYw#/registration

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to de-deprecate ChIPQC
messages.body_plain:
Thank you Lori.

I have pushed the fixes to the release branch as ChIPQC 1.48.1. The build
report is now clean, with install, build, and check all showing green.

Regards
Rory
bioconductor at starkhome.com

On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 12:23?PM Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at roswellpark.org>
wrote:

> The package was deprecated in 3.23 and removed from 3.24 you are correct
> that they currently do not appear or are building on devel.  Please push
> your changes to RELEASE_3_23 branch.  Once it builds there cleanly without
> ERROR we will undeprecate the package.
>
> Lori Kern
>

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Thank you Lori.

I have pushed the fixes to the release branch as ChIPQC 1.48.1. The build
report is now clean, with install, build, and check all showing green.

Regards
Rory
bioconductor at starkhom

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Request to de-deprecate ChIPQC
messages.body_plain:
Thank you we will undeprecate.


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________
From: Rory Stark <nospam at starkhome.com>
Sent: Friday, May 8, 2026 4:02 AM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Cc: Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at RoswellPark.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] Request to de-deprecate ChIPQC

Thank you Lori.

I have pushed the fixes to the release branch as ChIPQC 1.48.1. The build report is now clean, with install, build, and check all showing green.

Regards
Rory
bioconductor at starkhome.com<mailto:bioconductor at starkhome.com>

On Wed, May 6, 2026 at 12:23?PM Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at roswellpark.org<mailto:Lori.Shepherd at roswellpark.org>> wrote:
The package was deprecated in 3.23 and removed from 3.24 you are correct that they currently do not appear or are building on devel.  Please push your changes to RELEASE_3_23 branch.  Once it builds there cleanly without ERROR we will undeprecate the package.


Lori Kern

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Call for Stickers: EuroBioC2026 Hexwall
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,
EuroBioC2026 is planning a community sticker hexwall inspired by the incredible sticker collections featured at previous Bioconductor events. The wall will showcase package stickers from across the Bioconductor ecosystem as part of the conference space, celebrating the community's creativity and diversity.
If your package sticker is not yet included in the BiocStickers repository, now is the time to contribute!
?Submit here<https://github.com/Bioconductor/BiocStickers> by May 21, 2026
Find more information here: https://eurobioc2026.bioconductor.org/pages/hexwall.html
And if you know a package missing a sticker, whether from a colleague, student, collaborator, or one you use regularly, encourage them to join in too.

Best regards,
Laurah Ondari,
Bioconductor Community Engagement Support
Bioinformatician, International Institute of Tropical Agriculture (IITA)



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
